zh.Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾福布斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當John - of the Black Lip Forbes of Forbes於1332年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾福布斯時，他的父親John Deforbes30歲，母親Margaret Brough21歲。他於1359年與Elizabeth Kennedy of Dunure在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是7個兒子的父母。他於1394年在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾被列為justiciary and coroner。他去世於1406年11月20日在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾福布斯享年74歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾福布斯。

zh-hans.Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland: 苏格兰阿爾迪史爾福布斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John - of the Black Lip Forbes of Forbes于1332年出生在英国苏格兰阿爾迪史爾福布斯时，他的父亲John Deforbes30岁，母亲Margaret Brough21岁。他于1359年与Elizabeth Kennedy of Dunure在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是7个儿子的父母。他于1394年在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾被列为justiciary and coroner。他去世于1406年11月20日在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾福布斯享年74岁，埋葬在苏格兰阿爾迪史爾福布斯。

nameMeanings.name: Forbes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Forbes in Tullynessle, Aberdeenshire, so named from Gaelic forba ‘field, district’ + the locative suffix -ais. The placename is pronounced in two syllables, with the stress on the second, and the surname until recently reflected this. Today, however, it is generally a monosyllable.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Firbhisigh ‘son of Fearbhisigh’, a personal name composed of elements meaning ‘man’ + ‘prosperity’.
History: A family of this name (see 2 above) in Connacht was famous for its traditional historians, compilers of the Book of Lecan.

variants.name: Fawbush
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Forbes  , which ispronounced as two syllables in Aberdeenshire. Compare   Forbush  .

variants.name: Forbus
meanings: Altered form of Scottish   Forbes  , reflecting thetraditional pronunciation as two syllables.

variants.name: Forbush
meanings: Scottish: altered form of   Forbes  , which was traditionally pronounced as two syllables. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Altered form of German Vorbusch, a topographic name for someone living on the edge of a wood, from vor ‘in front of’ + North German Busch ‘wood, bush’.

variants.name: Fobbs
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Forbes  .

variants.name: Furbush
meanings: Probably an altered form of Scottish   Forbes  , which is traditionally pronounced as two syllables. Compare   Furbish  .

variants.name: Forbis
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Forbes  , reflecting the traditional pronunciation as two syllables.

variants.name: Fobes
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Forbes  .

variants.name: Forbess
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Forbes  , reflecting the traditional pronunciation as two syllables.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: the
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Dorothea  , also used independently.

nameMeanings.name: Black
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: chiefly from Middle English blak(e) ‘black’ (Old English blæc, blaca), a nickname given from the earliest times to a swarthy or dark-haired man. However, Middle English blac also meant ‘pale, wan’, a reflex of Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ with a shortened vowel. Compare Blatch and Blick . With rare exceptions it is impossible to disambiguate these antithetical senses in Middle English surnames. The same difficulty arises with Blake and Block .
Scottish: in Gaelic-speaking areas this name was adopted as a translation of the epithet dubh ‘dark, black-(haired)’, or of various other names based on Gaelic dubh ‘black’, see Duff .

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames directly or indirectly derived from the adjective meaning ‘black, dark’, for example German and Jewish Schwarz and Slavic surnames beginning with Čern-, Chern- (see Chern and Cherne ), Chorn-, Crn- or Czern-.

variants.name: Blagg
meanings: English: variant of   Black   with voicing of the final consonant.

variants.name: Blake
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which has two quite distinct etymologies. It is both from Old English blæc ‘black’ and from Old English blāc ‘pale, white’ it was thus originally a nickname given to someone with hair or skin that was either remarkably dark or remarkably light. It is now quite popular as a boy's name.

variants.name: Blackie
meanings: Scottish: diminutive of   Black  .
English: nickname for a person with dark eyes, from Middle English blak ‘black’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English blæc + ēage).

variants.name: Blackson
meanings: English: patronymic from the nickname or surname Black + -son. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Block
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a nickname for someone resembling a badger (Middle English broc(k), Old English brocc, of Celtic origin).

variants.name: Blacklock
meanings: English (northern): nickname for someone with dark hair, from Middle English blāk + lok(e) (Old English blæc + locc), although blāk might mean either ‘dark’ or ‘fair’ (see Black ). The meaning ‘dark hair’ is the most probable since this name contrasts with Whitlock .

variants.name: Blatch
meanings: English (Essex, but historically also in south and southwest England): nickname from Middle English blache, bleche ‘pale, pallid, of fair complexion’ (Old English blǣce).

variants.name: Blackett
meanings: English:
nickname for a person with dark or fair hair, from Middle English blak ‘black’ (Old English blæc) or ‘fair’ (Old English blāc) + hed, heved ‘head’. See also Black .
nickname from Middle English blak ‘black’ + hod ‘hood’.

nameMeanings.name: Lip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old Norse personal name identical in form with the vocabulary word hlíf ‘defence, protection’. In modern use it is often associated with Norwegian liv ‘life’, and is sometimes taken as a short form of Elisabet .

en.lifeSummary: When John - of the Black Lip Forbes of Forbes was born in 1332, in Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, John Deforbes, was 30 and his mother, Margaret Brough, was 21. He married Elizabeth Kennedy of Dunure in 1359, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 7 sons. In 1394, at the age of 62, his occupation is listed as justiciary and coroner in Aberdeenshire, Scotland. He died on 20 November 1406, in Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, at the age of 74, and was buried in Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John - of the Black Lip Forbes of Forbes est né en 1332, à Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Deforbes, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Margaret Brough, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Kennedy of Dunure en 1359, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils. En 1394, à l’âge de 62 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est justiciary and coroner à Aberdeenshire, Écosse. Il est décédé le 20 novembre 1406, à Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterré à Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als John - of the Black Lip Forbes of Forbes 1332 in Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Deforbes 30 und seine Mutter Margaret Brough 21. Er hat 1359 in Scotland Elizabeth Kennedy of Dunure geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne. 1394, als er 62 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als justiciary and coroner in Aberdeenshire, Scotland aufgeführt. Er starb am 20. November 1406 im Alter von 74 Jahren in Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland und wurde in Forbes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland begraben.

Lui sposato Elizabeth Kennedy of Dunure nel 1359, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli. ja.lifeSummary: John - of the Black Lip Forbes of Forbesが1332年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアフォーベズで生まれた時，父John Deforbesは30歳，母Margaret Broughは21歳であった。1359年にスコットランドでElizabeth Kennedy of Dunureと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子の親であった。1394年，62歳の時，スコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアでの職業はjusticiary and coronerと記録されている。彼は1406年11月20日にスコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアフォーベズで74歳で死去し，スコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアフォーベズにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John - of the Black Lip Forbes of Forbes(이)가 1332년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 포베스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Deforbes(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Brough(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1359년 스컷랜드에서 Elizabeth Kennedy of Dunure(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들을 두었다. 1394년, 62세의 나이로 그의 직업은 스컷랜드 압르디실르에서 justiciary and coroner이었다. 그는 1406년 11월 20일 스컷랜드 압르디실르 포베스에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 압르디실르 포베스에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Kennedy of Dunure em 1359, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos. ru.Battle, Duplin, Scotland: баттл, камрос коунти но. 2, Альберта, Канада
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John - of the Black Lip Forbes of Forbes родился в 1332 г. в местности форбесь, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Deforbes было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Brough, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Kennedy of Dunure в 1359 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей. В 1394 году, в возрасте 62 лет,  он работал по профессии justiciary and coroner в местности абирдиншир, Шотландия. Он умер 20 ноября 1406 г. в местности форбесь, абирдиншир, Шотландия в возрасте 74 лет и был похоронен в местности форбесь, абирдиншир, Шотландия.
